body:
Everything students need to create an account, sign in, and recover access starts here. Follow each section from top to bottom the first time through; returning users can jump directly to the relevant flow.
Before You Begin Make sure you have:
The student login URL for your environment — see Access URLs A valid email address you can check immediately A stable internet connection and an up-to-date browser Sign In with Email and Password This is the fastest path for returning students.
Open the student login URL. Click the Email field and type your registered email. Click the Password field and type your password. (Optional) Click the eye icon next to the password field to reveal what you typed. Click Sign In and wait for the redirect. What happens next:
You land on your Dashboard. If you were navigating to a specific page before login, you are sent directly there instead. If sign-in fails:
Double-check for typos — passwords are case-sensitive. If you see a message about email verification, follow the Verify Your Email section below. If you have forgotten your password, use Forgot Password. Sign In with Google Faster sign-in without a separate password.
Open the student login page. Click Continue with Google. A Google account picker opens — select the account you want to use. Approve the consent screen if prompted. You are redirected back to IDPicker and land on the dashboard. If the login window stays on the same page:
Your browser may be blocking popups. Allow popups for this site and try again. Make sure you are not using a private/incognito window with strict tracking prevention. Register a New Account New students follow a multi-step wizard. The exact steps depend on whether you received an invitation link.
Click Sign Up on the login page. Fill in your name, email, and a strong password. Password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one number, and one special character. Confirm the password and check the consent checkbox. Complete the interests and goals step. Fill out your profile (education level, gender, social links, occupation). Select an organization if prompted, or continue with invitation-prefilled values. Complete the human verification step. Click the final Register button. Tip: If you were invited by an advisor, your organization field may already be filled in. Do not change it unless instructed.
Common registration blockers:
Duplicate email → use a different address or try Forgot Password if you already registered. Consent checkbox not checked → scroll down and tick the box. Weak password → make sure it meets all strength requirements shown on screen. Verify Your Email After registration, IDPicker sends a verification email.
Open your email inbox (check your spam/junk folder too). Find the email from IDPicker with subject Verify your email. Click the verification link inside. Wait for the verification confirmation page. Once verified, you are directed to your dashboard or profile completion. If the link says it has expired:
Return to the login page. Enter your email and click Resend Verification Email. Open the new email and click the fresh link. Forgot Password Use this if you cannot remember your current password.
Open the login page and click Forgot Password. Enter your registered email address. Click Send Reset Link. Check your inbox and spam folder for the reset email. Reset Password From the Email Link After requesting a reset, you will receive a link by email.
Click the link from the IDPicker reset email. Wait a moment for the token to be validated. Enter your new password. Enter the same password again to confirm. Watch the strength indicator — make sure it reaches acceptable strength. Click Reset Password. Return to the login page and sign in with your new password. If the link says the token is invalid or expired:
Request a new reset link from the Forgot Password step above. Common Questions Situation What to do Correct password rejected Likely unverified email — resend verification from the login page Google login popup closed immediately Enable popups for the IDPicker domain in your browser settings Reset link opens but password field is grayed out Token expired — request a fresh reset link Registration stuck on verification Check spam folder; if email is over 30 minutes old, resend

body:
The Dashboard is the first thing you see after logging in. It gives you a bird’s-eye view of everything that needs your attention — assessments, notifications, forms, invitations, and program recommendations.
What You See on the Dashboard When you land on the dashboard, you will see several content blocks:
Block What It Shows What to Do Assessment Reports Your latest completed assessment output Download or view your report Assigned Forms Forms sent to you by your advisor or organization Open and respond Organization Invitations Pending invitations to join an organization Accept or Decline Notifications System and advisor announcements Read and act on relevant items Promoted Programs Highlighted university/career programs Browse and explore Random Programs Exploratory program discovery Click into any that look interesting Your First 5 Actions After Logging In Follow this sequence whenever you start a new session for maximum efficiency:
Check Notifications — Look for any new announcements from your advisor or platform. Open Assigned Forms — If a form is pending, complete it now before it slips away. Resume an Assessment — If you left an assessment in progress, the dashboard shows a resume prompt. Browse Recommendations — Click Recommendations in the sidebar to check your latest career or university matches. Explore Program Cards — Browse promoted and random programs to discover new options. Using the Assessment Reports Block Locate the Assessment Reports block on your dashboard. If a report is available, click the View or Download button. If the button appears locked or restricted: Your assessment may not be complete yet → go to Self Assessment in the sidebar. Your account plan may not include report downloads → check your subscription in Settings. Using the Assigned Forms Block Forms are created by your advisor and require your response.
Locate Assigned Forms on the dashboard. Click Open on any pending form. Complete all fields on the form page. Click Submit. You are returned to the dashboard and the form disappears from the pending list. Note: Submitted forms are removed automatically from the pending list. If a form disappears, it was already submitted or removed by the advisor.
Handling Organization Invitations Find the Invitations block on the dashboard. Click an invitation card to review the organization name and context. Click Accept to join or Decline to refuse. Your decision is saved immediately. Reading Notifications Locate the Notifications block. Click on any notification that looks relevant. It may take you directly to the related module (form, assessment, recommendation, etc.). After reviewing all, return to the main dashboard. Resuming an In-Progress Assessment If you started an assessment and did not finish it:
The dashboard shows a Resume prompt card automatically. Click Resume Assessment. You are taken back to exactly where you left off. Complete all remaining questions and click Submit. Exploring Program Cards Promoted programs are specifically featured by the platform or your organization. Random programs are surfaced for open exploration. Click any program card to view its details. To search and filter more precisely, open University Search from the sidebar.
Common Questions Situation What to do Dashboard appears empty on first login Normal — data populates as you complete assessments, receive forms, and get invitations A form I need is missing Check with your advisor — they may not have assigned it yet, or you may have already submitted it Report download button is greyed out Complete your assessment first, or check your subscription plan Recommendations block not visible It may only appear after your first assessment is complete

body:
Assessments are the core of IDPicker. Completing them unlocks your personalized career paths, university recommendations, and personality insights.
Starting a New Assessment From the left sidebar, click Self Assessment. Look at the available assessment cards (e.g., Personality, Career). Note: Your school or organization may assign custom assessments here as well. Click Start on the card you want to begin. Read the question instructions carefully. Select or type your answer, then click Next. Repeat until all questions are answered, then click Submit. Tip: Do not overthink your answers, especially for personality tests. Your first instinct is usually the most accurate!
Resuming an Incomplete Assessment Life happens. If you close your browser or navigate away, your progress is safely auto-saved.
Return to the Self Assessment page (or click the Resume card on your Dashboard). The assessment card will now show a Continue button instead of Start. Click Continue. You will pick up exactly where you left off. Complete the remaining questions and submit. Retaking an Assessment Some assessments allow you to retake them if you feel your situation has changed. If retakes are enabled for a specific test:
Open the completed assessment card. Click Retake. A confirmation prompt will ask if you are sure you want to overwrite your previous results. Confirm to proceed. Complete the new run and submit. Reviewing Your Results Once you submit an assessment, the platform instantly generates your results.
On the completed assessment card, click View Results. Review the summary and breakdown of your traits or aptitudes. Armed with this new data, head over to the Recommendations or Personality modules to see what the AI has unlocked for you! Important Note on Languages If you change your interface language (e.g., from English to Turkish) while an assessment is in progress, the platform may require you to confirm a reset of your current in-progress responses to match the new language schema. It is best to stick to one language until you submit!
Common Questions Situation What to do Recommendations or Personality menus are locked You must complete your core assessments first to unlock these areas The Start button is missing on a custom card The assessment may be closed, completed, or outside your organization’s allowed time window I lost progress unexpectedly Check if you switched languages mid-test. Otherwise, ensure you have a stable network connection

body:
Once you complete your core assessments, IDPicker’s AI generates a highly personalized list of recommendations. This guide explains how to use them to make informed decisions about your future.
Exploring Your Recommendations In the sidebar, click Recommendations. The system will load your profile-based track (either Career or University). Scroll through the recommendation cards. Click into any recommendation to open the detailed view, which explains why the AI believes this is a strong match for your specific assessment results. Tip: Keep a personal shortlist of the options that excite you the most to discuss later with your Advisor or AI Mentor!
Understanding Guidance Tracks IDPicker provides two distinct tracks depending on your current life stage. The platform automatically defaults to the most relevant track based on your profile:
Career Track: Recommended for high school students (Grades 9-12) focusing on long-term professional paths. University Track: Recommended for older students focusing directly on higher education program selection. Switching Between Tracks You are not locked into your default track. To explore the other side:
Scroll to the Guidance Track switcher section at the top of the page. Click either Switch to Career or Switch to University. Wait a moment for the save confirmation. The page will refresh with an entirely new set of tailored recommendations. Downloading Your Full Report If your account plan supports it, you can export your entire recommendation profile as a portable document.
Look for the Download My Report button at the top of the recommendations page. Click the button and wait for the preparation state to finish. A new tab will open containing a secure link to your generated PDF report. Note: If the download button is completely hidden, report generation is not enabled for your current account tier or organization policy.
Common Questions Situation What to do The recommendations feel inaccurate Your recommendations are strictly tied to your assessment answers. Consider retaking the assessment if you rushed through it. I am seeing universities but I want careers Use the Guidance Track switcher to manually change your focus I switched tracks but still see old content Wait for the save confirmation and refresh the page Report download failed Intermittent networks can interrupt generation. Wait 60 seconds and try again

body:
The Personality module visualizes your psychological traits and provides actionable advice on how to leverage your natural strengths.
Prerequisite: Complete the Assessment! Before you can see your personality breakdown, you must complete the Personality self-assessment.
From the sidebar, click Personality. If your assessment is incomplete, the screen will show a lock state. Click the prompt to Complete Assessment and finish it. Reading Your Radar Chart Once unlocked, the first thing you will see is your interactive Radar Chart.
Hover over the points on the radar axis to see the exact percentage scoring for each dimension. Identify the spikes (your strongest natural traits) and the dips (traits that require more conscious effort). The shape of the radar gives you an immediate, visual understanding of your behavioral footprint. Understanding Trait Cards Below the chart, you will find detailed cards for every measured trait.
Each card breaks down your score into highly actionable sections:
Trait Title & Score: Your baseline metric. Short Description: A one-sentence summary of what this trait means. Detailed Interpretation: Deep dive into how this score manifests in your daily life and studies. Actionable Advice: Concrete, practical steps on how to use this trait to your advantage (or mitigate its downsides). Downloading Your Personality Report Just like recommendations, you can export your psychological profile to share with parents, mentors, or for your own records.
Click Download Report at the top or bottom of the analysis page. Wait a few seconds for the document to generate. Click the secure link that opens in a new tab to save the PDF. Common Questions Situation What to do The entire page is locked You must complete the Personality Assessment first The chart loads but the trait cards are blank A network drop may have occurred. Reload the page The report button works but no file opens Generation might have temporarily timed out. Wait a moment and retry

body:
The Mentor Hub is your central communication space. Here, you can seek advice from specialized AI personalities or chat directly with your assigned human advisor.
Starting a Chat with an AI Mentor IDPicker provides multiple AI personalities, each tuned for specific types of advice (e.g., strict academic planning, empathetic career counseling, technical skills building).
In the sidebar, click Mentor. Browse the grid of available mentor cards to find a personality that fits your current question. Click Select/Open on your preferred mentor. Once the chat interface opens, type your question into the message box at the bottom. Click the Send icon. The AI will respond in real-time, contextually aware of your personality and recommendation profile! Quota Warning: Depending on your subscription, you may have a monthly chat quota. A progress bar usually indicates how many messages you have left. Use your prompts wisely!
Resuming a Recent Chat You do not have to start over every time.
In the Mentor Hub, look for the Recent Chats list. Click on a recent thread. The chat history will load, and you can pick up the conversation exactly where you left off. Chatting With Your Human Advisor If your account is linked to an organization or school, your human advisor manages your progress. You can message them directly here.
On the main Mentor page, look for the Advisor section. You will see your advisor’s name, last known activity, and a red badge if you have unread messages. Click the Chat with Advisor button. Type and send your message. When your advisor replies, you will be notified on your dashboard. Note: If the advisor section says “No advisor linked,” your account operates independently and this feature is disabled.
Common Questions Situation What to do I clicked a mentor but the chat didn’t open Refresh the page and try again; verify your network is stable The Advisor chat button is greyed out You do not currently have a human advisor linked to your account My messages stopped sending You have likely hit your chat quota limit. Check your subscription settings

body:
The University Search module is your comprehensive database for discovering higher education programs worldwide. This guide explains how to use the search engine efficiently to build your target list.
Basic Keyword Search In the sidebar, click University Search. Wait a moment for the filter panel and the default results area to load. Click the Search input box at the top. Type a university name (e.g., Harvard) or a program keyword (e.g., Computer Science). Pause briefly — the search runs automatically as you type to prevent unnecessary reloading. Review your refined results. Power Filtering To drill down into specific criteria, use the filter panel on the left (or in the top dropdowns, depending on your device).
1. By Category (Field of Study) Click the Category dropdown and select your overarching field, such as Engineering, Arts, or Medicine. The results will instantly filter to programs mapped to that category.
2. By Degree Type Looking for a Bachelor’s, Master’s, or PhD? Click the Degree dropdown to restrict results to exactly what you need.
3. By Language of Instruction If you want to study entirely in English in a non-English speaking country, this filter is life-saving. Select the instruction language to filter out programs you cannot attend.
Warning: Mixing too many strict filters at once (e.g., Aerospace Engineering, PhD, Turkish language, in Germany) will likely return zero results. If you get an empty page, clear your filters one by one until results reappear.
Managing Pagination (Load More) When reviewing results:
Scroll to the bottom of the current page of university cards. Click Load More (or use the pagination numbers 1, 2, 3…) to fetch the next set. You can change how many cards load at once by tweaking the Page Size control (e.g., 6, 12, 24). Note: Any time you apply a new filter or type a new keyword, you are automatically bumped back to Page 1 so you do not miss top results.
How to Compare Programs Efficiently When building your shortlist, we recommend focusing on these core factors displayed on every card:
University Name & Program Name Degree Level Language of Instruction Tuition Cost (if available) Duration (years) Country / City Keep a notebook or spreadsheet open alongside IDPicker to save your top targets!
Common Questions Situation What to do The search bar feels laggy Input is deliberately “debounced” (delayed) by a few milliseconds so it does not overwhelm the server while you type. Just pause when finished! My results completely disappeared You probably applied an overly strict combination of filters The card order suddenly changed When you apply a new filter, a brand new sorted list is retrieved from the database

body:
The Resources module acts as your personal digital library. Whenever your advisor, school, or organization shares a helpful document, spreadsheet, or PDF, it lands here.
Browsing the Resource Library From the main sidebar, click Resources. Wait a second for your personalized file grid to load. You will immediately see files that are explicitly assigned to you, as well as globally visible files shared by your organization. Searching by Keyword Know exactly what you are looking for?
Click the Search bar. Type a matching keyword from the file’s title, description, or original file name. The list will instantly filter to matching documents. Filtering and Sorting Files If your organization has shared hundreds of files, use these controls to narrow them down:
File Type Filter: Only want to see PDFs or Excel sheets? Select the exact format. Size Filter: Select Small, Medium, or Large files (helpful if you are on a slow cellular connection). Visibility Filter: Choose between All, Globally Visible, or Directly Assigned. Sort By: Change the ordering from Date Added, Alphabetical Name, or File Size. View Mode: Toggle between the spacious Grid view or the compact List view depending on your preference. Downloading a Shared File Locate the card or row of the file you need. Click the Download button. Your browser will handle the secure download. Check your computer’s Downloads folder when it finishes. If a download fails: Check your internet connection first. If it still fails, refresh the entire page to ensure your login session has not quietly expired, then try downloading again.
Common Questions Situation What to do My advisor said they shared a file, but I cannot see it They may have forgotten to assign it directly to your email. Ask them to verify the visibility settings on their end. The file list keeps shuffling when I click things The Sort By option applies instantly whenever a filter changes A file card is missing a description Advisors are not strictly required to type out extensive descriptions when uploading

body:
Sometimes things break or you encounter a feature you do not understand. The Help Center is your direct line to the IDPicker platform support team.
Opening a New Support Ticket In the sidebar, click Help Center. Make sure the New Ticket tab is selected. Click the Subject line and type a brief, clear summary (e.g., Cannot download personality report). Select the matching Category (Technical, Account, Billing, Feature, General, or Other). Select a Priority (Low, Medium, High, Urgent). Note: Please only use Urgent for complete account blockers (like being totally unable to log in). In the Description box, provide as much detail as possible. Click Submit and wait for the success flash. How to Write a Ticket That Gets Fixed Fast The support team wants to help, but they cannot see your screen. Provide these details to speed up resolution:
Exactly what page or module you were on. The exact text of any error message shown. What you clicked before the error happened. What you expected to happen, vs what actually happened. Tracking Your Submitted Tickets Once submitted, you can monitor the status of your request without sending duplicate emails.
Click the My Tickets tab at the top of the Help Center. Find the issue in your list. Review the Status badge to understand current progress: Status Badge What it Means Pending Received, sitting in the queue waiting for an agent In Progress A support agent is actively investigating right now Resolved The agent has implemented a fix or provided an answer Closed The issue is fully completed and archived If an agent replies requiring more info, you will be notified!
Common Questions Situation What to do I clicked submit but got a red error Check that you actually wrote a Subject and Description. Both are required! I submitted my ticket but my list is empty You are probably still on the New Ticket tab. Switch back to My Tickets What category do I pick for login issues? Select Account, unless you are specifically facing a payment/subscription issue, then pick Billing

This guide explains how students identify free-plan limits and use upgrade paths.
Understand Feature Limits Free-plan limits can apply to:
Mentor chat usage Assessment/recommendation usage counters Full report download availability Where Students See Limit Signals Open a quota-sensitive module (Mentor, Assessments, Recommendations). Locate quota indicator or blocked state. Read remaining usage and limit label. Upgrade Flow from Feature Context Reach a gated/limited action. Click Upgrade or equivalent CTA. Follow the presented flow for plan upgrade. Note:
Dedicated subscription page availability may vary by deployment. In-context upgrade prompts are currently the reliable path. What to Do When Subscription Menu Exists but Page Is Missing Stay in module where limit appears. Use in-module upgrade CTA. Confirm post-upgrade access after completion. Common Student Questions Q: I can read recommendation cards but cannot download full report. A: Full report export can be plan/availability controlled.
Q: I see subscription in sidebar but route fails. A: Use in-module upgrade actions until dedicated route is enabled in your deployment.
Q: How do I check remaining usage? A: Check quota indicators shown in quota-controlled features.

This guide explains how students edit profile, manage parent links, switch guidance track, and delete account safely.
A) Edit Profile (Click-by-Click) In sidebar, click Profile. Click Edit Profile. Update fields as needed: First name Last name Bio Location Job title Gender Instagram URL X URL Click Save. Wait for success confirmation. If save fails:
Recheck required fields and URL formats. Retry once after page refresh. B) Switch Recommendation Track from Settings In sidebar, click Settings. Locate Guidance Track section. Click switch action (Career/University). Wait for saved confirmation. Open Recommendations and confirm new mode. C) Link Parent Account Stay on Settings page. Locate Parent Linking section. Click parent email input. Enter parent email. Click Send/Request Link. Wait for confirmation. Possible outcomes:
Linked immediately Pending until parent-side confirmation flow D) Remove Parent Access In Parent Links list, locate linked parent row. Click Unlink. Confirm action. Verify parent is removed from linked list. E) Delete Student Account (High Risk) In Settings, open Delete Account area. Click Delete Account. Read warning carefully. Enter required confirmation word exactly. Click final delete confirmation. Wait for logout/redirect. Best practice:
Export/save anything needed before deletion. Common Student Questions Q: Parent link request sent but not linked yet. A: Link may stay pending until parent confirmation path is completed.
Q: Track switched in settings but recommendations unchanged. A: Re-open Recommendations or refresh once.
Q: Delete button did not proceed. A: Confirmation word is strict; type exactly as shown.

body:
As an Advisor, your platform access is tailored to your operational role. This guide covers how to get logged in securely and how to interpret your main dashboard.
1. Advisor Login Depending on your organization’s setup, you will access the platform either via a custom organization link or the main advisor portal. (See Access URLs if you are unsure).
Open the Advisor Login page. Enter your registered Email and Password. Click Sign In. The system initializes your specific role and permission set, then drops you onto your Dashboard. Why is my dashboard missing certain modules? If you are a “Sub-Advisor,” your access is strictly limited to the permissions your primary Advisor delegated to you. If you cannot see a module, you do not have permission for it.
2. Setting Up Your Account (First Time) If you were invited to join as an advisor or sub-advisor by your organization lead:
Check your email for an invitation from IDPicker. Click the Setup Password link in that email. You will be taken to a secure token-validation page. Enter a strong new password, confirm it, and submit to activate your account. Return directly to the login page and sign in. 3. Password Reset Flow If you have forgotten your password:
On the login page, click Forgot Password. Enter your advisor email address. You will receive a reset link. Click it, set a new password, and verify the strength indicator. If the link says it is expired, immediately request a new one from the login page. 4. Understanding Your Dashboard The Advisor Dashboard serves as your daily command center.
KPI Widgets (Top Row): These blocks show quick metrics regarding your linked students, such as total student count, recently completed assessments, and pending forms. Assigned Forms Block: Immediately see which of your forms currently have pending responses from students. Main Navigation (Sidebar): Use the sidebar to quickly jump between managing Students, organizing Groups, distributing Resources, or handling Support/Chat. Tip: If your platform supports multiple languages, use the language toggle in the header to switch your interface language instantly.

body:
The Students module is your CRM for managing the individuals you advise. You can view their progress individually, assign them to groups, or import hundreds of students at once.
Searching and Filtering Your Roster When you open the Students module from the sidebar, you see your entire authorized roster.
Search: Use the search bar to find a student quickly by name or email. Filter: Narrow down your list using the drop-down filters. You can filter by: Assigned Groups Chat status (e.g., awaiting reply) Assessment signals (e.g., completed, missing) Joined date or location Sort: Click the column headers (Name, Email, Joined Date) to reorder the list. Viewing Student Details Click on any student row or card to open their detailed profile. Here you get full context on their background, activities, and current plateau.
Managing Group Assignments To help organize your communications, you can assign students to Groups right from the roster.
From the Students list, select the checkbox next to one or more students. Click the Assign to Group action button. Check the boxes for the target groups. Click Save. The membership updates instantly. (Note: See the Groups Management guide for how to create groups in the first place).
Bulk Importing Students (Excel) If you have a large cohort starting simultaneously, do not add them one by one. Use the Bulk Import tool.
On the Students page, click Bulk Actions > Import. Prepare your .xlsx or .xls file. Your spreadsheet must contain these three exact column headers in the first row: email first_name last_name Upload the file. The system will parse the file and show a Preview Table. Select the rows you wish to actually import, or select all. Click Confirm Import. Wait. The system will summarize successes and any skipped rows (failures). Important Import Rules File Limits: There is a maximum file size limit (usually a few megabytes). Duplicates: If an email already exists in the system, that row will be skipped/flagged. Group Assignment: Imported students are not automatically placed in a group upon import. You must assign them afterward. Common Questions Situation What to do I uploaded an Excel file but the preview is totally blank Make sure your file is a valid .xlsx and exactly matches the required lowercase header names (email, first_name, last_name) The filters say “0 students found” but I know they exist You likely have conflicting filters applied. Clear all filters and reapply them one by one I just imported students; how do I message them all? Create a Group for the new cohort, assign them, and then use the Notifications module to broadcast a message

body:
This guide covers three distinct operational flows that advisors handle daily: approving new students, managing multidirectional chats, and setting up organizational assessments.
1. Managing Enrollment Requests Depending on your organization’s configuration, students who register may not join your roster automatically. They may land in an approval queue.
In the sidebar, click Enrollment Requests. A list of pending student accounts will appear. Click into a request to review the individual’s profile to verify they belong to your organization. Click Approve to admit them (which grants them full platform access and adds them to your Students roster). Click Deny if they do not belong. 2. Using Family Chat IDPicker recognizes that guidance is a collaborative effort between the advisor, the student, and the parents.
In the sidebar, click Family Chat (or navigate to the Chat module). You will see different message threads categorized by scope: Student Threads: Direct 1-on-1 chats with the student. Parent Threads: Direct chats with the linked parent. Family Threads: Multi-party threads involving both the student and the parent(s). Select a thread, type your message in the composer, and hit Send. A red badge will appear next to names when they reply. Tip: If a parent thread is disabled, it means the student has not yet approved a parent linkage request on their end.
3. Custom Assessments While IDPicker provides built-in psychological and career assessments, your organization may want to issue bespoke quizzes (e.g., a “Math Placement Exam” or “Mid-Year Feedback”).
In the sidebar, click Assessments. Click Create Assessment (or clone an existing template). Build your questions (Multiple Choice, Rating Scales, Short Answer). Configure the visibility parameters (e.g., Which groups get this? Does it expire?). Publish the assessment. It will immediately appear on the targeted students’ Self Assessment dashboard. Common Questions Situation What to do The Approve/Deny buttons in Enrollment are missing Your account might be a restricted sub-advisor, or the request is already processed. Check the “Resolved” tab I sent a family chat but the parent did not reply Parents receive email notifications about unread messages, but they still have to log in to read them. Mention it during a phone call I need to fix a typo in a Published Assessment Once an assessment starts receiving answers, structural edits may be locked. Fix typos immediately before distributing, or duplicate the assessment and pause the old one

body:
The Forms module allows advisors to create custom questionnaires, surveys, or data-collection sheets, distribute them to specific students, and track the responses in one place.
Understanding the Forms Lifecycle Every form moves through a specific lifecycle:
Draft: The form is being built and is invisible to students. Published: The form is actively collecting data and is visible to assigned students on their dashboard. Archived: (If applicable) The form is no longer accepting new responses, but old data is preserved. 1. Creating and Publishing a Form In the sidebar, click Forms. Click Create New Form. Enter a Title and a helpful Description. Use the form builder interface to add your specific fields (e.g., text inputs, dropdowns, checkboxes). Click Save as Draft while you are still working on it. When you are completely finished, select your target audience (groups or specific students) and change the status to Publish. Preview Before Publishing Always click the Preview action before publishing! This lets you see the form exactly as a student will see it, ensuring that your required fields and visual layout behave as expected.
2. Managing Existing Forms You can manage your forms from the main Forms list view:
Search & Filter: Use the search bar to find a specific form by title, or filter to see only Drafts or Published forms. Edit: If a form is still a draft, you can freely edit its structure. Once an active form begins receiving responses, structural edits may be heavily restricted to prevent data corruption. Delete: Select the delete action to permanently remove the form. Note: Doing this may also delete associated response records. 3. Reviewing Student Responses Once your form is published and students begin filling it out, you need to review their answers.
Locate the active form in your Forms list. You will see a counter indicating the number of Total Responses. Click the Responses button. A table will load showing a list of every student who submitted the form and the timestamp. Click on an individual response row to drill down and read the specific answers provided by that student. Common Questions Situation What to do A student says they submitted, but my response count is not updating The interface does not automatically live-refresh. Manually refresh the page to load the latest data from the server The system prevents me from editing a form The form is either locked due to your permission policy, or it is locked because it is Published and actively receiving responses I accidentally deleted a form Form deletion is permanent. Always export response data before taking destructive actions

body:
Groups are an essential organizational tool. By grouping students—whether by graduation year, specific study tracks, or custom campaign objectives—you can distribute forms, share resources, and send notifications much more efficiently.
1. Creating a New Group In the sidebar, click Groups. Wait for the current groups list to load. Click the Create Group button. Enter a clear, descriptive Group Name (e.g., “Class of 2026 - Engineering Track”). (Optional) Add a short description for your own internal reference. A student selector will appear. Select the specific students you want to add to this group right now. Click Save. Your new group appears in the list. Best Practices for Naming Groups Be Consistent: Use a standard naming convention (e.g., [Year] - [Major/Track] - [Cohort]). Be Specific: “Group A” becomes impossible to manage after a few months. Review Often: Clean up and delete groups when a cohort graduates or finishes a campaign. 2. Editing an Existing Group Need to rename a group or add/remove members?
Find the target group in the list and click to open it. Click Edit. Update the name, description, or adjust the member checkmarks. Click Save to apply the changes instantly. Note: You can also add students directly to groups from the Students List by using the bulk action menu.
3. Deleting a Group When a group is no longer needed:
Open the target group or click its action menu (...) in the list view. Click Delete. A confirmation prompt will ask if you are sure. Click Confirm. Review the roster to verify the group is gone. Warning: Deleting a group does not delete the student accounts inside it. It only removes the organizational tag grouping them together.
Common Questions Situation What to do I cannot find a specific student to add to a group Check your permission scope; if you are a sub-advisor, you can only group students you have explicitly been granted access to The Delete button is grayed out or missing Your sub-advisor permissions do not allow you to delete groups. You can only view or manage members I saved the group but the member count looks wrong Re-open the edit dialog and verify that you actually checked the boxes next to the student names before hitting save

body:
The Notifications module is your mass-communication tool. Instead of sending hundreds of individual emails, use notifications to broadcast platform-native announcements, reminders, and alerts directly to student dashboards.
1. Creating a New Broadcast In the sidebar, click Notifications. You will see a history of your past broadcasts. Click Create Notification. Enter a clear, attention-grabbing Title. Type your Message Body. Keep it concise but ensure all necessary links or action items are included. Select your Target Type: All Students: Broadcasts to every single student in your organization. Use sparingly. Selected Users: Allows you to hand-pick individuals. Selected Groups: The most common option. Targets a pre-made cohort (e.g., “Seniors”). If you chose Users or Groups, a selection interface will appear. Pick your targets. Click Send (or Create). The notification is immediately dispatched and will appear in your history list. 2. Reviewing Delivery and Effectiveness Unlike email, platform notifications allow you to track engagement. In your notification history list, you can verify:
Target Type: A quick reminder of who received the broadcast. Created Date: Exactly when it went out. Total Recipients: The absolute number of unique students who received the ping. Read Count: How many students have clicked on the notification to read the full body. Unread Count: How many students have ignored or not yet seen the alert. 3. Filtering Your Broadcast History If you need to check what you sent months ago:
Apply the Target Type filters (e.g., show me all messages sent to “All Students”). Use the search input to find a specific keyword or campaign title. Review the historical Read Counts to determine which types of subject lines get the most engagement from your roster. Common Questions Situation What to do The read rate is extremely low Give it 24 hours. Students only see notifications when they actively log in to their dashboard I accidentally sent a notification to the wrong group Platform notifications are instant and generally cannot be un-sent. Send a follow-up correction immediately to the same wrong group The “Selected Groups” option is greyed out You cannot target groups if you have not created any groups yet. See the Groups guide

body:
The Resources module operates as a secure file-sharing library. Advisors can upload helpful materials—like study guides, university brochures, or policy PDFs—and control exactly which students are allowed to see them.
1. Uploading a New Resource In the sidebar, click Resources. Click the Upload Resource button at the top of the screen. Click to browse your device, or drag-and-drop your target file. Fill in the metadata: Name: (Required) A clear, recognizable title. Description: (Optional) Add context on why this file is helpful. Category: (If enabled) Tag the file to keep the library organized. Choose your initial visibility setting (Visible to All or Assigned Only). Click Upload. Wait for the upload to complete and verify the file appears in your library list. 2. Managing Visibility and Assignments Uploading a file is only half the job. You must ensure the right students can see it.
Globally Visible: Any student linked to your organization can see and download this file. Assigned Only: The file is hidden by default. Only students you explicitly select will see it. How to Assign a Hidden File to Specific Students Locate the file in your Resources list. Click the action menu (...) and select Assign to Students. A student selector modal will appear. Check the boxes next to the individuals (or groups) you want to share this with. Click Save Assignment. 3. Organizing and Finding Files As your library grows, use the built-in filters to find what you need quickly:
Search Bar: Quickly locate files by their title or description text. File Type Filter: Narrow the list to show only documents, spreadsheets, or images. Visibility Filter: See exactly which files are currently hiding behind “Assigned Only” status. Sort By: Reorder the grid by the date added, the alphabetical name, or the raw file size. View Mode Toggle: Switch from the visual Grid View to the dense List View if you are managing dozens of files at once. 4. Deleting an Outdated Resource When a syllabus or brochure is no longer accurate:
Locate the file card. Open the action menu and click Delete. Confirm the permanent deletion prompt. Note: Disabling visibility is often safer than deleting if you think you might need the file again next year. Common Questions Situation What to do My file upload failed instantly The file is likely larger than the allowed maximum upload limit, or it is a blocked file type (like .exe) I assigned a file but my student says they cannot see it Double-check that you selected their exact profile in the assignment modal, and verify their account is active in your roaster The list and grid views are sorted differently The Sort option applies globally, but if you switch views, you may need to click the sort dropdown again to re-apply it

body:
If you are a primary Advisor managing a large organization, you can invite sub-advisors (like assistants or specialized tracking counselors) to help you. This guide explains how to invite them and safely restrict their access.
1. Creating a New Sub-Advisor In the sidebar, click Sub-Advisors. Click the Create Sub-Advisor button. Fill in their identity details: Full Name Email (This will be their login) Set their Assigned Scope: If you want them to text only 12th graders, assign them explicitly to the “12th Graders” Group. They will not be able to see any students outside of that group. Configure their Permission Set: Decide exactly which modules they can see (e.g., enable Students and Chat, but disable Forms and Settings). Click Save / Create. The system will email them an invitation link to set up their password. 2. Editing Sub-Advisor Permissions If a sub-advisor’s role changes, you can adjust their access on the fly.
Open the Sub-Advisors module. Click on the target sub-advisor’s name. Scroll down to the Permission Matrix. Toggle specific module access on or off as needed. You can also change which exact groups they govern here. Click Save. The changes will apply the next time they log in or refresh their page. Validating Their Access If you want to be completely sure you set the permissions correctly:
Ask the sub-advisor to log in while you watch, OR Temporarily log in using a designated “test” sub-advisor account setup with the identical permission matrix. Verify that restricted modules are completely hidden from the sidebar. 3. Revoking Access (Deactivation) If a sub-advisor leaves your organization, you must revoke their access immediately to protect student privacy.
Open their sub-advisor record. Change their status to Inactive (or click the Revoke Access button, depending on your UI version). Click Save. The sub-advisor is immediately logged out of all active sessions and cannot sign back in. Common Questions Situation What to do My sub-advisor cannot see any students Check their Assigned Scope. If they are not assigned to any groups, and not given “All Students” access, their roster will be blank A sub-advisor sees too many modules You granted them too many checkboxes in the Permission Matrix. Uncheck the modules you want hidden and Save I deactivated an account but they are still logged in While the system attempts to kill active sessions immediately, a hard refresh on the sub-advisor’s browser will forcefully enforce the new locked state

body:
Parents use the IDPicker Parent Panel to monitor their child’s progress, review recommendations, and communicate with advisors. This guide covers how to get your account set up and securely log in.
1. Registering a New Parent Account If you are new to the platform:
Open the Parent Registration URL. Fill out the registration form with your Email, Full Name, and a Strong Password. Check the consent checkbox at the bottom of the form. Click Register. The system will tell you to check your email for a verification link. Open your inbox, click the verification link, and you will be routed automatically back to the platform to log in. Verification Link Expired? If you clicked the email verification link and it says expired, simply go to the login page, enter your email, and click the Resend Verification button.
2. Setting Up an Invited Account Often, schools or advisors will create a parent account on your behalf and send you an invitation.
Open your email inbox and find the invitation from IDPicker. Click the Setup Password link in the email. The browser will open a secure setup page. Enter a strong new password that you will remember, and confirm it. Submit the form, then sign in with your email and new password. 3. Signing In (Click-by-Click) Open the Parent Login page. Click the Email field and enter your registered parent email address. Click the Password field and enter your password. Click Sign In. You will land directly on your Parent Dashboard. If login fails, double-check your password spelling. Passwords are case-sensitive.
4. Resetting a Forgotten Password If you forget your password, you can easily recover your account securely.
On the login page, click Forgot Password. Enter your parent email address and click Send Reset Link. Check your email (and spam folder) for the reset message. Click the link in the email. Enter a new password, confirm it, and submit. Return to the login page and sign in with the new password. Common Questions Situation What to do I try to log in but get a “Parents Only” error You are trying to log into the Parent portal using a Student or Advisor email address. Make sure you use the parent-specific link I reset my password but it won’t let me submit Your new password is likely too weak. It must meet the minimum length requirements (usually 8+ characters with a mix of numbers/letters) I stopped receiving emails Check your spam/junk folder and ensure noreply@idpicker.com is whitelisted in your email provider

body:
Once you are logged in, the Parent Dashboard is your main command center. It provides an at-a-glance summary for every child linked to your account.
1. Understanding Dashboard Blocks When you land on the dashboard, you will immediately see:
Linked Children Cards: A dedicated card for each of your linked students showing their name, avatar, and quick progress percentage. Progress Snapshots: High-level metrics showing how many assessments have been completed across your family. Advisor Activity Indicators: Quick badges showing if you have unread messages from your child’s advisor. Link Student Form: The input box used to request access to a new child’s account. 2. How to Link Your Child Before the dashboard shows any meaningful data, you must link your parent account to your child’s student account.
On the dashboard, locate the Link Student form. Enter the exact email address your child used to register their student account. Click Send Link Request. What happens next depends on the system configuration: Instant Link: If your email matches records sent by the school, the student card appears immediately on your dashboard. Pending Request: The system sends a notification to your child’s student dashboard. They must click “Approve” before you can see their data. (For more details on managing this link, see the Linked Student Guide.)
3. Opening a Student Card To dive into the specific details for one child:
Locate their card on your dashboard. Click anywhere on the card (or click View Student). The platform will navigate to the Student Detail Page, where you can see their full assessment results, progress, and recommendations. Got Multiple Children? No problem. Simply use the Link Student form again to request access to your second or third child. They will each appear as a distinct, separate card on your dashboard.
4. Refreshing Stale Data If you know your child just finished an assessment, but your dashboard still says “Incomplete”:
Look for the circular Refresh icon on your dashboard. Click it and wait a moment. The system will bypass caching and pull the latest, live data directly from the servers. Common Questions Situation What to do My dashboard has no student cards at all You need to use the “Link Student” form to connect your account to your child’s account first I linked my child but their stats are entirely blank They probably have not started their self-assessments yet. Give them a gentle nudge! The system says “Pending Request” for days Your child has not opened their dashboard to click the “Approve Parent Link” button. Remind them to log in

body:
IDPicker requires explicit linking between a Parent account and a Student account to protect student privacy. This guide explains how to establish that link and what you can (and cannot) see.
1. Requesting a Link by Email If your child’s school has not automatically linked your accounts, you must initiate the request from your dashboard.
Sign in to your Parent Dashboard. Locate the Link Student form. In the input box, carefully type the exact email address your child used to register. Click Send Link Request. Depending on the platform settings, you will experience one of two outcomes:
Instant Link: The student appears immediately on your dashboard. Pending Link: The request is sent to your child. They must log in to their student dashboard and click “Approve” before you can see any of their data. 2. Opening the Linked Student Profile Once a link is established, your child appears as a card on your dashboard.
Click View Student on their card. The platform opens their specific profile page. You can now review their progress summaries, recommendation insights, and recent advisor activity. To see live updates, use the Refresh button on this page. If you have multiple children on the platform, each has their own dedicated card and profile page.
3. Removing Access (Unlinking) If an account was linked by mistake or is no longer needed:
Open the linked student’s detail page. Scroll to the bottom and find the Remove Access (or Unlink) button. A confirmation prompt will appear. Click to confirm. You will be returned to your dashboard, and their card will be permanently removed. 4. Understanding Parent Permissions The Parent Panel is designed for monitoring and support, not authoring.
What You CAN Do: View assessment progress and milestone completions See selected career/university recommendation insights Preview and download their Journey Map Chat with their advisor and see unread message indicators What You CANNOT Do: Edit their test answers or take assessments for them Modify their profile data (like email or password) See private, restricted journal entries or private notes intended only for the advisor Common Questions Situation What to do I sent a link request but nothing happened Ask your child to log in and approve the pending request The student detail page fails to load Your session may have timed out, or the link was revoked by the student. Refresh the page and check your dashboard Can I reset my child’s password for them? No. Security policies require the student to use the “Forgot Password” link on the normal student login page

body:
Once you open a linked student’s profile from your dashboard, you gain access to powerful tracking tools and direct communication lines with their educational team.
1. Reviewing Progress and Insights At the top of the student detail page, you will see their Basic Profile (name, age, grade). Below this, the page is broken down into insight blocks:
Assessment Progress: Quickly see which required psychological or educational assessments your child has completed, and which are still pending. Recommendations Summary: A high-level view of the career paths or universities the AI has suggested for them based on their results. Mentorship Activity: See if your child has been utilizing the AI mentors or checking in with their human advisor. 2. Generating the Journey Map The Journey Map is a comprehensive, exportable report that synthesizes your child’s entire profile into a single document. It is highly useful for college applications or external counseling.
Scroll to the Journey Map section on the detail page. Select the specific Data Points you want included in the report. You can check or uncheck categories like: Demographics & Education Psychological Traits Career Ambitions Social, Family & Health Metrics Click Preview to see a structural mockup of the data before committing to the download. Once you are satisfied with the included data, click Generate & Download Journey Map. The system will process the file (this takes a few seconds) and open a secure PDF download link in a new tab. 3. Chatting with the Advisor If your child’s school has enabled Family Chat, you can communicate directly with their assigned advisor from this page.
Locate the Advisor Chat panel on the student detail page. A red badge will indicate if you have unread messages. Click to open the secure chat thread. You can read past messages, ask questions, and coordinate support strategies directly through the platform. 4. Managing Subscription Plans If IDPicker is offered to you as a direct-to-consumer product (rather than through a school), you control the billing.
Scroll to the Subscription area. Review your child’s current active plan tier. Compare available upgrades (e.g., unlocking full PDF reports or unlimited mentor chats). Click to initiate the secure stripe/checkout flow to upgrade their account instantly. Common Questions Situation What to do The Journey Map preview is totally empty You must select at least one data point checkbox (like “Education”) before the preview will generate The Journey map downloaded, but pages are blank Your child may not have completed enough assessments to populate those specific sections yet I see a high unread count, but no new messages in the Advisor Chat Try refreshing the page. In rare cases, the unread count may reflect messages sent strictly to your child (Student-Advisor thread) rather than the Parent-Advisor thread

body:
The Admin Panel is the highest level of access in IDPicker, designed for platform owners and high-level operations staff. This guide covers secure access and daily monitoring routines.
1. Secure Admin Login Accessing the admin portal requires navigating to a specific, protected route.
Open the Admin Login URL (often /admin/login). Enter your authorized admin Email or Username. Enter your Password. Click Sign In. Once your high-level authorization cookie is validated, you are routed directly to the global dashboard. Forgotten Password? If you lose your password, click Forgot Password, enter your email, and use the secure reset link sent to your inbox. Because of the high privilege level of this account, password strength requirements are strictly enforced.
2. Daily Dashboard Checks The admin dashboard provides a live pulse of the entire platform’s health and activity. Make it a habit to review these four areas every time you log in:
Global KPI Cards: Check total active users, new registrations today, and total completed assessments. Recent Assessments Table: A live feed of the newest data coming into the system from across all organizations. Registration Trend Chart: Look for sudden spikes or drops in daily sign-ups. System Activity Panel: A generalized event feed tracking high-level system actions. 3. Monitoring AI Token Usage Because the AI Mentor and generation features consume API tokens, monitoring usage is critical for cost control.
Scroll to the Token Usage section on the dashboard. Adjust the date range filter to look at the last 24 hours or the last 7 days. Review the charts for Input Tokens (prompts sent to the AI) and Output Tokens (responses generated by the AI). If you notice a massive, unexplained spike in usage, immediately cross-reference the exact timestamp with the Realtime Logs to identify potential abuse or infinite loops. Common Questions Situation What to do I log in successfully, but the dashboard widgets are completely blank High-level data queries can take a moment to resolve. Wait 5 seconds and refresh the page I click a link and keep getting bounced back to the login page Your secure session cookie has likely expired. Clear your browser cookies for the domain and sign in again My token usage seems artificially high Check if an organization recently ran a bulk import and triggered automated report generations for hundreds of students simultaneously

body:
As a platform administrator, you control the foundational structure of IDPicker. This module allows you to manage the top-level Organizations (schools, agencies) and manually audit the individual users beneath them.
1. Organization Management Organizations are the containers that hold Advisors, Students, and Parents. Before onboarding a new school, you must create their Organization record.
In the sidebar, click Organization Management. Click Create Organization. Fill in the organization’s public profile details (Name, Location, Branding if applicable). Assign the Primary Admin (the head advisor or IT contact for that school). Click Save. The organization is now active and its admin can begin inviting students. To edit or delete an organization, use the action menu next to its name in the list view. 2. Auditing Student Accounts While Advisors manage their own rosters, Super Admins have global visibility over every student in the system for support and auditing purposes.
Open Student Management from the sidebar. Use the global search bar to locate any student by name or email across all organizations. Expand a student’s row to view their full diagnostic detail, including: Email verification status Premium subscription status Total login/activity counters Global Deletion: If requested for GDPR/CCPA compliance, you can hard-delete a student from this menu. Use extreme caution and always confirm before executing. 3. Managing Parent Accounts Parents exist independently but are relationally linked to students.
Open Parent Management. Search and filter to locate a specific parent profile. Expand their detail view to manually manage their relationships: you can forcefully Link them to a student by ID, or Unlink a relationship if a mistake was made. You can also manually trigger a new invitation email from this panel if a parent claims they never received theirs. 4. Safety Checklist Before Deletion When an organization ceases operation, or a user requests account deletion, follow these steps to prevent data corruption:
Verify Dependencies: Ensure there are no active financial subscriptions tied to the account. Export Data: If required by your SLA or compliance policy, export their assessment records before deletion. Execute: Delete the dependent users first (e.g., Students/Parents) before deleting the parent Organization container. Confirm: Rely on the system’s confirmation dialogue, but always double-check the target ID. Common Questions Situation What to do I created an Organization but the head Advisor cannot log in Reopen the organization record and double-check you spelled their Admin Email correctly. Check their verification status I linked a parent manually but they cannot see the student They may need to refresh their session. Also, verify that the student account is actively fully registered, not just a draft I accidentally deleted a user Hard deletions via the Admin panel are permanent and cascade through the database. You will need to restore from a database backup if recovery is critical

body:
Not all administrators need full access to every setting. This module allows you to create internal staff accounts and rigorously defend your organization’s security posture by enforcing least-privilege access.
1. Creating an Admin User Open the Admin Management page. Click Add Admin. Fill in the required identity fields: Email (Must be unique across the platform) Username First / Last Name Set a strong temporary password. The new admin should change this immediately upon their first login. Select their foundational Role (e.g., Super Admin vs Content Admin). Click Save. 2. Managing the Permission Matrix The true power of IDPicker’s internal security is the Permission Matrix, which configures access on a highly granular, per-module basis.
Locate the target admin in your active list and click to open their Permission Panel. For every module (Users, Assessments, Support, Financials, etc.), set their specific access scope: Write: Full read/edit/delete authority. Read: Can view the data, but cannot change it. None: The module is completely hidden. If your platform uses multi-tenancy, you can also restrict their scope to specific Organizations rather than global access. Click Save Permissions. Important: If the subadmin is currently logged in, you must instruct them to refresh their page or log out/in for the new permissions to apply to their session token immediately.
3. Subadmin Self-Auditing If you are a subadmin and believe you are missing access to a module you need to perform your job:
Open the Subadmin View from the sidebar. Review your effective permission summary. It lists exactly what you are authorized to do. If there is a discrepancy, reach out to your Primary Admin to update your matrix. Common Questions Situation What to do A subadmin can see the User list, but the “Delete” button is missing You granted them “Read” access to the Users module. They need “Write” access to execute deletions or edits I updated permissions but they say nothing changed The subadmin must manually trigger the “Refresh Permissions” action or fully re-authenticate to generate a new permission token Can subadmins create other subadmins? Usually no, unless they have explicit “Write” access on the Admin Management module itself

While Advisors can create localized forms for their specific students, Super Admins control the global Assessments and global Forms that apply across the entire IDPicker ecosystem.
1. Creating a Global Assessment In the sidebar, open Assessments. Click Create Assessment. Provide the foundational metadata: Title & Description: Make these public-friendly. Category: (e.g., Psychological, Placement, Feedback). Enter the visual builder to add questions. Supported formats include: Rating Scales (1-5, 1-10) Multiple Choice (Single or Multi-select) Short/Long Answer text fields Yes/No Booleans Configure required flags and save. Testing is Mandatory: Before distributing a global assessment to thousands of students, use a test student account to verify the logic, spelling, and user experience.
2. Bulk Importing Assessments If you already have established paper-based psychometrics, you can import them directly rather than typing hundreds of questions manually.
From the Assessments list, click the Upload action. Prepare a .csv or .xlsx file according to the platform’s strict import schema (Columns: Question Text, Type, Options, Required). Select and upload the file. Review the Import Preview. The system will flag any rows with invalid schemas or missing options. If the preview is clean, click Confirm Import. 3. Global Forms Management Global Forms are distinct from Assessments—they are used for temporary data collection, surveys, or registration funnels.
Open the Forms module. Create your form schema and mark it Published. To view incoming data safely, click the Responses button next to the active form. You can see the aggregate response count and drill down into individual submission rows. Common Questions Situation What to do My Assessment Import throws a “Broken Options” error You likely uploaded a Multiple Choice question row but left the Options column blank in your spreadsheet. Fix the sheet and re-upload I see 100 responses but the counter only says 50 Refresh the list view. The counter caches to improve performance and may need a manual refresh, or check if you have an active date/pagination filter applied Should I use a Form or an Assessment? Use Assessments for core psychological/educational profiling that impacts Recommendations. Use Forms for temporary surveys and unstructured data collection

body:
The core intelligence and language of IDPicker is not hard-coded; it is entirely manageable via the Admin Panel. This allows you to tune the AI Mentors and translate the UI on the fly without requiring engineering deployments.
1. Managing System Prompts The system prompts dictate exactly how the AI Mentors behave, what tone they use, and how they analyze student data.
Open the Prompts module. Select the specific category and subcategory you want to tune (e.g., Mentor Persona > Academic Strict). Open the target prompt template. Edit the instructions in the code editor. CRITICAL: Do not delete dynamic variables (e.g., {{student_name}} or {{assessment_score}}). Removing these breaks the AI’s contextual awareness. Validate the syntax and click Save. The updated prompt version is now live instantly. 2. Interface Translations (Localization) If you need to fix a typo in the UI, or launch the platform in a brand new language, use the Translations manager.
Open the Translations module. Use the filters to select a specific Namespace (e.g., student_dashboard) and Locale (e.g., es-ES for Spanish). Use the search bar to locate the specific key or the incorrect phrase. Click Edit, type the corrected translation, and save. If you are adding a completely new phrase required by a recent UI update, use the Create Translation action to add the missing key. Cache Warning: Translations are heavily cached in users’ browsers for speed. Force clear your local browser cache to verify the UI updated correctly.
3. Configuring Mentor Personalities You can directly manage the roster of AI Mentors available to students.
Open the Mentor Personality section. You can tweak a mentor’s localized name, short description, and their internal Expertise Array (which dictates what tags they are allowed to give advice on). Ensure you save your changes before leaving the page. Common Questions Situation What to do I saved an updated Prompt but it threw a “Template Syntax Error” You likely broke a brace bracket around a variable, like typing {student_name}} instead of {{student_name}}. Fix the brackets and retry I updated a Translation but the user still sees the old text Instruct the user to perform a “Hard Refresh” (Ctrl+F5) to clear their browser cache, or wait for the CDN cache to expire The AI Mentor is going “off script” Revisit your System Prompt. Ensure the instructions are explicit, using commands like ‘DO NOT EVER suggest…’. AI models require explicit, rigid boundaries

body:
This module covers the operational workflows for global customer support and gating access to the platform via formal approvals.
1. Managing Support Tickets When a student or advisor submits a Help Center request, it routes to this global queue.
Open Support Tickets. Immediately filter the queue by Priority: Urgent and Status: Pending to catch blockers. Click into a ticket to read the user’s description and reproduction steps. Once you have a fix or answer, change the Status (e.g., from In Progress to Resolved). Type your official response in the Resolution Message field. Click Save. The user will see your response on their local Help Center dashboard. Note: If a ticket transition fails silently, re-open it. You likely forgot to fill out a mandatory “Resolution Message” field before trying to change the status to Resolved.
2. Generating Global Notifications While Advisors notify their specific students, Super Admins can push alerts globally across all active organizations on the platform.
Open Notifications. Click Create Notification. Choose your target scope extremely carefully: All Users: Every active student, parent, and advisor. (Use for major platform maintenance warnings only). All Students: All students regardless of organization. Selected Groups: Specific subsets if necessary. Write your Title and Message, then hit Send. Once sent, verify it appears correctly in your broadcast history. 3. Demo Approvals and Admission Criteria If IDPicker is operating a gated launch or pilot program, you must manually approve access.
Open the Demo Approvals or Admission Criteria queue. Review the incoming request details submitted by prospective users or schools. Click Approve to officially provision their workspace and trigger the welcome email sequence. Click Reject if they do not meet compliance standards (you can usually append an internal note explaining the rejection). Common Questions Situation What to do I accidentally sent a Global Notification to everyone Platform notifications are instant and cannot be recalled. If the error was severe, send an immediate correction broadcast apologizing for the error I approved a Demo request but the user got an error logging in Verify the organization template mapped to their approval was fully provisioned. Sometimes the backend provisioning queue takes 30-60 seconds Can I assign a ticket to a specific subadmin? If your Support module supports assignment flags, use the ‘Assignee’ dropdown inside the ticket detail before saving

body:
For technical administrators, this section acts as your infrastructure dashboard. Here you can monitor server health, catch bugs as they happen, and control the financial levers of free usage.
1. Reviewing the Status Module Open the Status page from the sidebar. Review the core service health blocks (Database, API, AI Services, Storage). If any component is flagged yellow (Degraded) or red (Offline), immediately cross-reference your infrastructure alerts. The status page helps isolate whether a reported bug is a local user error or a widespread server outage. 2. Using Realtime Logs When a user reports a persistent crash, the Realtime Logs are your best diagnostic tool.
Open the Realtime Logs module. Click Start Stream to watch live events hit the backend in real-time. Apply filters: Set log level to ERROR or FATAL to strip out the noise. Set the source filter if you are tracing a specific module (e.g., stripe-webhook or ai-generation). Look for sudden bursts or cascading error statements. These can be exported and sent to your engineering team. 3. Free-Use Policy Management If your platform offers a “freemium” tier, you need to govern exactly how much free API processing the average user can consume before hitting a paywall.
Open Free Use Management. Review the current limit caps (e.g., Max Free AI Chat Messages = 10, Max Free Reports = 1). Adjust the limits up or down depending on your current marketing strategy or token budget. Click Save. The updated thresholds immediately apply to the gating logic on the student apps. 4. Certificates and Compliance Documents Open the Certificates or Application Documents queue. Review pending document validations submitted by users (e.g., proof of high school graduation or language proficiency). Apply review decisions (Verify / Reject) based on your governance policy. Add internal audit notes to every rejection for compliance tracking. Common Questions Situation What to do The Realtime Logs view is completely empty Ensure you clicked “Start Stream”. Also check if your exact filter combination is too restrictive (e.g., looking for FATAL logs on an entirely healthy system) I see a massive spike in ERROR logs but the Status page says “Healthy” A specific edge-case feature might be crashing (generating logs) without actually taking the core database offline. Investigate the log stack trace immediately I updated the Free-Use limit but students say they are still blocked Global platform settings often rely on multi-stage caching. Verify the environment variable propagated correctly, or wait 5 minutes for the cache TTL to expire

body:
Use these playbooks when users report blocked workflows.
Playbook 1: Cannot Sign In Symptoms:
Invalid credentials error Redirect loop to login Role mismatch message Checks:
Confirm correct role URL. Confirm account exists and is active. Confirm email verification status. Clear cookies and retry login. Escalate when:
Multiple users fail with same error pattern. Login endpoint responds with server errors. Playbook 2: Form Cannot Submit Symptoms:
Submit button fails silently Validation error persists Response not saved Checks:
Verify required fields are filled. Verify field type input is valid (email/number/date). Retry submit after reload. Validate response route ID is correct. Escalate when:
Reproducible failure on same form for multiple users. Backend returns repeated 5xx errors. Playbook 3: Parent-Student Link Not Visible Symptoms:
Link request sent but student not listed Link status remains pending Checks:
Verify student email exact match. Verify student account exists in same environment. Ask both users to refresh after completing link steps. Escalate when:
Link status remains pending beyond expected SLA. Playbook 4: Ticket Queue Not Updating Symptoms:
Status change appears unsaved Resolved tickets reappear as pending Checks:
Reopen ticket and confirm latest status in detail view. Ensure required resolution message fields are populated. Save and refresh list. Escalate when:
Persistence fails across multiple ticket IDs. Playbook 5: Notification Delivery Issues Symptoms:
Notification sent to unintended audience Expected recipients missing Checks:
Verify target type. Verify selected user/group IDs. Re-send with a small test target first. Escalate when:
Delivery metrics mismatch persisted target selection. Playbook 6: Dashboard Performance Degradation Symptoms:
Slow initial load Chart/table timeout Intermittent failed fetches Checks:
Reduce date range/filter complexity. Compare behavior in another module. Inspect status/realtime logs for error bursts. Retry after short interval. Escalate when:
p95 latency remains elevated across modules. Logs indicate systemic backend faults. Incident Handoff Template When escalating, include:
Role and panel (student/advisor/parent/admin) Exact URL/route Timestamp and timezone Reproduction steps Error text and screenshot Expected vs actual behavior Whether issue is single-user or widespread

body:
Access and Accounts 1. I cannot log in. What should I check first? Confirm you are using the correct panel URL for your role. Re-enter username/email and password carefully. Check whether your email verification is pending. Use password reset if you suspect credential mismatch. 2. Why do I get redirected back to login? Most common causes:
Expired or invalid auth cookie Wrong role token used on another panel Backend session/token invalidation Fix:
Sign out. Clear browser cookies for the app domain. Sign in again with correct role URL. 3. I completed registration but cannot access dashboard. Verify email from inbox link. Retry login after verification. If link expired, use resend verification. Student/Parent Linking 4. Parent cannot see linked student immediately. Linking may remain pending until both sides complete required actions.
Parent submits link request with correct student email. Student side confirms matching flow where required. Parent refreshes dashboard. Assessments, Recommendations, and Reports 5. Why is a module locked? Some modules unlock only after required assessments or permissions are completed.
6. Report download button is missing. Report export availability may depend on:
Completion state Plan/quota state Backend report generation status Support and Operations 7. Ticket status did not update. Reopen ticket detail. Ensure required fields (for example resolution note) are present. Save again and refresh list. 8. Notification reached wrong users. This is usually a target-selection issue.
Verify target type (all/users/groups). Validate selected recipients before send. Localization 9. Language changed but some text stayed in old language. Refresh page once. Confirm selected language is persisted. Check missing translation key in translations module (admin/advisor). Performance 10. Dashboard feels slow. Reduce heavy filters/date ranges. Reload once to clear stale state. Check status/log modules for backend latency spikes.

IDPicker Documentation The complete guide to mastering the IDPicker career and university guidance platform.
🚀 Getting Started Set up your account, learn the access URLs, and view the quick start guide.
🎓 Student Panel Complete workflow for students from assessments to university search.
